name: Kosh Tili
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the flour, granulated sugar, and salt.
2. Add eggs, yogurt, milk, and a dash of vanilla or rosewater to the dry ingredients. Mix well with a spoon until combined.
3. Transfer the mixture to a lightly floured work surface and knead until smooth (about 5 minutes).
4. Divide the dough into four equal parts. Place one piece on a floured board and roll out to an thickness of 1/8 inch.
5. Cut the rolled-out dough into strips that are about 1-1/2 or 2 inches wide and 6-8 inches long using a knife or crimp-edged cutter.
6. Make a 1-1/2-inch slit in the middle of each strip.
7. Fold one end of each strip through the slit, pulling it out on the other side.
8. Repeat with the remaining dough strips.
9. Heat oil for deep-frying to the appropriate temperature (the recipe does not specify a frying temperature).
10. Fry 1 or 2 dough strips at a time until they puff up and turn golden brown.
11. Drain the fried fritters in a colander, then place them on paper towels and sprinkle with powdered sugar.
12. Serve and enjoy! Makes 8 to 12 servings.
